links.label: a centurion ... beseeching him ... come and heal
I will come / Jesus went with them
I am not worthy that thou shouldest come / enter under my roof
speak the word only / say in a word, and my servant shall be healed
a man under authority ... Go, and he goeth ... Come, and he cometh ... Do this, and he doeth it
he marvelled ... I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel
his servant was healed / found the servant whole

name: The centurion's servant
notes: At Capernaum a centurion's servant is healed at a distance, told by Matthew and Luke. The heart of it is identical in both: "I am not worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof," the soldier's reasoning from authority, and the Lord's wonder — "I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel." Matthew tells it directly, the centurion himself speaking; Luke gives the fuller account, in which the centurion sends the elders of the Jews and then friends to carry his words, and is never face to face with Jesus. Matthew reports by attribution what Luke unfolds through the messengers — the same exchange, closer or further off. Luke's elders pleading his worth (7:4-5) and Matthew's word on many coming from east and west (8:11-12) are each the one writer's own, left unlinked.
